assertions.expectation: Explains that surge upgrades require extra compute capacity (maxSurge creates NEW nodes before draining old ones)
assertions.reason: Explicitly explains surge nodes can't be created due to compute quota limits and describes how surge creates replacements requiring extra quota

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting maxUnavailable >0 instead of relying on maxSurge (drains first, then creates — uses less quota)
assertions.reason: Recommends drain-first upgrade with maxSurge=0 and maxUnavailable=1, explaining it drains nodes before creating replacements requiring zero extra quota

assertions.expectation: Warns that maxUnavailable >0 means reduced capacity during upgrade — some pods won't have nodes
assertions.reason: Clearly states the trade-off of 'temporary capacity loss during upgrade' and mentions 'capacity dip' when using drain-first approach

assertions.expectation: Suggests temporarily scaling down non-critical deployments to free up quota for surge nodes
assertions.reason: Provides Option 3 with specific kubectl commands to scale down non-critical workloads and includes this in the recommended combined approach

assertions.expectation: Mentions that reducing maxSurge to 1 minimizes extra quota needed (slower but fits within constraints)
assertions.reason: Option 2 shows maxSurge=1 configuration and explains it 'requires quota for only 1 additional node instead of the larger surge'

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to update surge settings (maxSurge=0, maxUnavailable=1 for zero-extra-capacity mode)
assertions.reason: Provides exact gcloud command with maxSurge=0 and maxUnavailable=1 parameters for drain-first configuration

assertions.expectation: Recommends doing the upgrade in off-peak hours when fewer pods are running
assertions.reason: Option 4 specifically addresses scheduling during off-peak hours and includes it in the recommended combined approach

assertions.expectation: Mentions requesting a temporary quota increase as a faster alternative to a permanent increase
assertions.reason: Does not mention requesting a temporary quota increase - only provides alternatives that work within existing quota constraints
